palette_hint: Background #F4F6F9, primary text #1A1D23, accent teal #0E8A7D, secondary accent amber #D97706 for highlights and warnings.
type_style: Headings in system-ui bold 600-700 weight, body and table cells in monospace (Courier New / system mono) for numeric alignment, overall feel is clinical and data-dense.
description: VoltWatch is a battery-technology comparison site that lets consumers and engineers evaluate 14 lithium-ion, LFP, and solid-state battery models across chemistry, capacity, cycle life, cost-per-kWh, and safety ratings.
constraints: Comparison tables must be max-width 1200px centered, with sticky thead using position:sticky top:0 and background #1A1D23 with white text, row height 36px, zebra striping alternating #FFFFFF and #EDF1F7.
All numeric columns (capacity, voltage, cycle count, price) must be right-aligned and rendered in monospace font with consistent 12px padding-right so digits align vertically across rows.
Custom radio buttons and checkboxes use CSS-only appearance:none technique with a 16x16px border-radius:3px box, teal #0E8A7D fill on :checked, and a 2px solid #1A1D23 unchecked border.

Form inputs on the selector page must have a uniform height of 38px, border 1.5px solid #CBD2DA, border-radius 4px, and shift border to #0E8A7D on :focus with no outline, laid out in a 3-column CSS grid with 24px gap.
Cells flagged as best-in-class receive a left border 3px solid #0E8A7D and background #E6F4F2; cells flagged as below-average receive left border 3px solid #D97706 and background #FEF3E2.

page_specs.overview: Landing page introducing VoltWatch with a bold 48px heading 'Compare Every Watt' and a 280-word editorial summary of battery chemistry types (Li-ion NMC, LFP, solid-state) currently covered. Below the text, a 5-column summary grid lists each of the 14 battery models as a card-style table row showing model name, chemistry badge, nominal voltage, and a teal CTA link to the full specs table.
page_specs.specs-table: The core comparison page: a horizontally scrollable dense table with 14 rows (one per battery model) and 18 columns including Model, Manufacturer, Chemistry, Nominal Voltage (V), Capacity (Ah), Energy Density (Wh/kg), Operating Temp Range, Max Continuous Discharge Rate (C), Weight (kg), Dimensions (mm), IP Rating, BMS Included, Warranty (years), and Price (USD). Sticky header row in dark #1A1D23, zebra striping, best-in-class and below-average cell highlights applied per the constraint rules. A filter bar above the table contains three inline radio-button groups (Chemistry: All / NMC / LFP / Solid-State; Voltage: All / 12V / 24V / 48V; Capacity: All / <50Ah / 50–200Ah / >200Ah) using CSS-only custom radios.
page_specs.charge-cycles: A detailed table comparing cycle-life performance for all 14 models at three depth-of-discharge levels: 80% DoD, 50% DoD, and 20% DoD, with columns showing expected cycle count and projected calendar life in years at each DoD level. A second sub-table below shows charge efficiency (%), self-discharge rate (%/month), and C-rate at which cycle life was tested. All numeric cells are right-aligned monospace; cells exceeding 3000 cycles at 80% DoD are highlighted as best-in-class per palette rules.
page_specs.cost-analysis: A four-section page starting with a cost-breakdown table: columns for Model, Purchase Price (USD), Usable Capacity (kWh), Cost per kWh (USD), 10-Year TCO (USD), and Levelized Cost per Cycle (USD/cycle), all right-aligned monospace. Below that, a 14-row payback-period table compares each model against a baseline lead-acid reference at residential solar (0.12 USD/kWh) and commercial (0.22 USD/kWh) electricity rates. Amber highlights flag models with TCO above $800/kWh; teal highlights flag models below $300/kWh.
page_specs.selector-form: A guided selection form helping users find the right battery using a 3-column CSS grid layout with 24px gutter. Fields include: Application Type (select dropdown: Residential Solar, EV Conversion, Marine, Off-Grid Cabin, Industrial UPS), Required Capacity (number input, Ah), System Voltage (radio: 12V / 24V / 48V), Max Budget (number input, USD), Preferred Chemistry (checkbox group: NMC / LFP / Solid-State / No Preference), Minimum Cycle Life (select: 500 / 1000 / 2000 / 3000+), Operating Temperature Min and Max (two number inputs, °C), BMS Required (radio: Yes / No / Either), and a textarea for additional notes. Required fields show a red-left-border #DC2626 via :invalid:not(:placeholder-shown) CSS state; valid filled fields show teal-left-border #0E8A7D via :valid. A results summary table at page bottom shows only matched models.
